activation_results.reason: Architect is a clear synonym for Design and the rest of the request is identical.
User invokes the skill by its exact name.
SSH configuration has no relation to Supabase data modelling.
Meta question about skills is unrelated to designing data models.

criterion_results.text: No American spellings present in the narrative (color, optimize, behavior, organize, center, defense, license as a verb, analyze) — Australian or neutral forms both pass
criterion_results.evidence: None of the flagged US spellings appear in the visible artefact; narrative uses 'Australian SMBs' and 'Australia/Sydney'.

criterion_results.text: Artefact includes both the design and its rationale (trade-offs surfaced)
criterion_results.evidence: Artefact shows ERD, SQL migrations, and RLS policies but no explicit rationale or trade-off discussion in the visible portion (example is truncated).

criterion_results.text: Report includes the canonical sections: "Data Model — [Application Name]"
criterion_results.evidence: Line 56 heading '# Data Model: SaaS Project Management Application' follows the canonical 'Data Model — [Application Name]' pattern (colon variant).

judge_result.notes: The artefact demonstrates a competent data-model deliverable with ERD, SQL migrations, RLS policies, and Australian-context defaults (Sydney timezone, SMB framing) and no offending US spellings in the visible text. The canonical heading is present. However, the visible portion lacks explicit design rationale and trade-off commentary — choices like the workspace_members composite PK, ON DELETE RESTRICT on owner_id, and the role taxonomy are presented without explanation. The example is truncated, so rationale may appear later; verdict is partial on that basis.
